entry.title: Harnessing Clean Core and SAP Business AI:Key Partners innovate with extensions during Hack2Build
entry.content:
Introduction

18 Partners across the globe responded to the Hack2Build initiative on Clean Core to build innovations using Clean Core principles using SAP Business AI capabilities, and to drive customers’ success on their RISE with SAP journey.
When companies are pursuing the cloud journey, it is essential to follow a clean core strategy which facilitates the companies to use the latest release, minimize or eliminate modifications, and run with cloud-compliant extensions and customizations. SAP’s Clean Core strategy is one of the cornerstones of the RISE with SAP motion as it safeguards the path for our joint customers to a truly modern Cloud ERP approach. Along with SAP Business AI, it allows for unprecedented automation, decision making, and use experience in a trustful, reliable, and relevant way. Customers derive substantially increased cloud transformation value by SAP Business AI as it is only available in the cloud.
SAP offers a powerful toolset with both low-code and pro-code development tools to build your Cloud ERP extensions. The partners leveraged the recently announced SAP Build Code, the Generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, to experience the power of application development and build clean core extensions. 

About Hack2Build

Hack2Build, ‘Beyond a Hackathon’ is an initiative from Partner Ecosystem Success, which brings together the creativity and speed of prototyping to the partners. Partners validate their ideas with SAP during the ‘Hackathon phase’  and build prototypes in 7 days. Partners are supported with expertise from SAP, to move from prototyping to the ‘Build phase’, to develop their market-ready solution. As a part of the Hack2Build initiative from June 6-13,2024, 18 partners submitted their clean-core use case to build new applications and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition.

Partner Companies in Hack2Build on Clean Core Extensions - June 2024

Feedback were collected as part of the demo jam sessions. In general, partners enjoyed the fast paced innovation and the support provided by SAP. 96% of partner participants responded that beyond prototyping, that they are willing to develop the full scope of their clean core extension solution in about 10 weeks and drive for customer adoption.

The participating partners across APJ, EMEA and Americas were bifurcated into two groups and were supported by BTP product management, BTP Architects, S/4HANA experts and the larger Partner Ecosystem Success teams. Two groups of winners emerged during the demo jam held on June 13, 2024. The next sections highlight the winning solutions.

Winner Solutions

Group 1 constituted of 8 Partners and the following winners were picked by the jury panel.

Winner: IBM

Clinical Trails Patient Engagement Smart App was developed as an extension to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud to address multiple issues being faced by the clinical trial patient volunteers, such as insufficient details to register for the study, address change of schedule, support for volunteers with disabilities and other issues. IBM set out to leverage Gen AI Hub capabilities, SAP Build Code, to develop a clean core CAP application to help the clinical trial volunteers.​ The application showcased how the volunteers can get their clinical trial related queries answered with the help of digital assistant, how it enable volunteers to keep track of their appointments, address adverse weather forecast to reschedule as per their needs. Any out-of-range parameters from the medical report are presented to the volunteers in easily understandable language. ​

Clinical Trails Patient Engagement Smart App by IBM

1st Runner Up : Engineering Group

Optimizing invoice management process with AI: AI Invoice Processing (AIP) supports invoice management by addressing key challenges such as OCR readability issues, incomplete data, inconsistencies between invoices and purchase orders, and the risk of undetected fraud. Leveraging the power of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), AIP automates and enhances electronic invoicing, unlocking the value of AI. It also integrates seamlessly with Public Administration systems for efficient and scalable invoice management, handling invoices from various channels including suppliers and government entities, using AI-powered OCR for higher accuracy. AIP excels in data extraction and anomaly detection using AI and supports user interactions through a chatbot. Flexible deployment options allow for either independent use or seamless integration with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and SAP Vendor Invoice Management(VIM) by Opentext, optimizing operational efficiency and ensuring regulatory compliance. Engineering's composable solution incorporates BTP-based Fiori web applications on a microservice architecture in a Cloud Foundry environment, supported by standard BTP services like SAP HANA Cloud, SAP AI Core, SAP Document Information Extraction, and SAP Build Work Zone.

AI Invoice Processing (AIP) by Engineering Group

2nd Runner Up: FPT Software Co. Ltd.

mySalesPro by FPT Software is a mobile assistant solution for B2B Sales team in companies to help them with quick access to products, services, promotion details, multimedia show-cases, simplifies sales rebate and sales contract creation, and provides customer management on their computer tablet or mobile phone. mySalesPro enables AI-driven sales rebate programs generation, FAQ, etc. The application leverages SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) integrated with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) on SAP HANA Cloud. Frontend Architecture is based on SAP Fiori and using SAP Mobile Services for the mobile app which was built using Cross-Platform SAP MDK SDK.

mySalesPro by FPT Software

Group 2 constituted of 9 Partners and the following winners were selected.

Winner: Seidor


Airport Billing Solution is a niche solution for airports to prevent revenue leakage by accurately tracking and charging hours spent by airplane carriers and the various services used while the airplane is in the airport. It caters as charging solution for capturing  and processing all the data related to aircraft arrival, parking, towing, and departure ensuring accuracy, reliability, and efficiency in managing the entire life cycle. it is designed to support automation of the billing processes, providing control and monitoring tools for real-time tracking, and ensuring transparency and traceability of financial transactions. Seidor developed the solution as a side-by-side extension, using SAP Build Code to generate the base code. The solution leverages Generative AI Hub to suggest best tariffs. The Sales and Financial Documents are created in SAP S/4HANA, Private edition.

Airport Billing Solution by Seidor

1st Runner Up: NTT Data

ProcureGen is a Procure to Pay extension solution for Healthcare developed by NTT Data. The current systems support dispensing of standard medical, surgical, Pharma items for patient from internal stores, or provisioning of services internally in the wards. But, if there is a need to procure patient specific products/services like bespoke orthotics or home care services, then department staff rely on typically disjointed processes, leading to inefficiencies, errors in order processing, and delays in delivery, which ultimately affect patient care quality. ProcureGen is developed as a smart patient-facing ordering and goods receipt application with Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources(FHIR) based Integration with Clinical systems, Catalog-Based Ordering, Mobile Compatibility, Data Privacy and Security regarding patient data, with Financial Process Integration with SAP S/4HANA to handle the financial aspects from procurement to payment. It is built as a side-by-side extension by using standard SAP APIs for integration with SAP S/4HANA. SAP Build Code, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP HANA Cloud, Document Information Extraction are some of the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) services leveraged to build the application. Key User extensibility in the core SAP S/4HANA system was used to display an additional field Patient ID in the standard SAP Fiori app for Purchasing transactions which will provide opportunities for patient level reporting within the core SAP system.

ProcureGen by NTT Data

2nd Runner Up : Athenium

The Sophia Weigh Bridge Assistant, developed by Athenium Consulting, is aimed at replacing legacy, manual, and paper-based weigh bridge processes with a digital solution. It is used to track and control inbound and outbound deliveries that depend on weigh bridges as a critical control. It offers enhanced capabilities such as geo-location tagging, signing on glass and collecting invaluable supply chain data applicable to inbound and outbound deliveries. All artefacts such as documents, signatures and supply chain data, gathered using the Sophia Weigh Bridge Assistant, are made available in a customer portal application where customers can get real time insights or interrogate historical data. The Sophia Weigh Bridge Assistant supports mobile (IOS and Android), tablet and PC platforms. Leveraging artificial intelligence, the captured supply chain data can be used to identify operational or safety improvements. The solution is aimed at, but not limited to, the needs of Oil and Gas, Mining and Agriculture industries. The Sophia Weigh Bridge Assistant is clean core compliant, deployed as a side-by-side extension using SAP BTP services such as SAP Workzone, SAP Build Code development, SAP Build Apps for mobile needs, SAP Event Mesh along with other services like SAP Build Process Automation, Identity Authentication, PostgreSQL.

Sophia Weigh Bridge Assistant by Athenium Consulting

Judges

A diverse panel of experts were brought together to be in the panel of judges. The panel evaluated the partners pitch during the demo jam and scored them on selected criteria on the clean core extension theme. Thanks to our judges for their valuable time and evaluation.
- Karthik Thirunavukkarasan: Principal Product Manager, SAP Build Cross Services & Extensibility
- Kathrin Hettel : Vice President, Strategic Cross Portfolio & AI Partnerships, Partner Ecosystem Success
- Luciano Ravenna : Global Vice President, Partner Solution Success
- Niklas Siemer : Senior Product Manager SAP BTP
- Pavel Penaz : Head of SAP BTP and AI , Partner Solution Success
- Rajeev Gollapudi : Chief Product Manager, SAP BTP Product Management
- Rohan Shetty : Vice President, Head of S/4HANA Cloud Delivery
- Rudolf Held : Vice President, Global Head of SAP Co-Innovation Lab

Way Forward

The Hack2Build initiative provides a continuum for participating partners to transition from the 'Hack Phase' to the 'Build Phase'. SAP teams are actively involved in the subsequent phase to assist partners in developing the full scope of their solutions. We anticipate that these partner solutions will drive customer adoption of their innovations, thereby supporting the clean core strategy.

entry.title: Why SAP Build Code?
entry.content:
Dear community,

The past few months have been very encouraging to us as the SAP Build Code team. A lot of customers and partners have adopted and continue to adopt SAP Build Code. It is very heartening to see this traction.

Along the way, some customers have approached us with their questions. We realized that these may be worth addressing to a larger audience via a blog. So here goes…

Why did SAP introduce SAP Build Code?

Cloud-native application development comes with its set of complexities. In fact, IDC found out that in any project, developers spent less than 20% of their time writing code. 80% of the time was spent in researching and making decisions about the development frameworks, architectural patterns, security measures, runtime platform, and monitoring, lifecycle management, CICD, observability requirements for that project.

A typical set of app development, runtime, platform, and monitoring tools that a team must choose from before starting a new project.

To make this simple for our SAP customers, in TechEd 2023, we launched the SAP BTP Guidance Framework which comprises guidance for architects, developers and administrators.  

SAP BTP Developer’s Guide

 How do customers implement the SAP BTP Developer’s Guide for their own projects?

This is where SAP Build Code comes in. By using SAP Build Code, you can build your cloud-native extensions (highlighted in yellow in the diagram above) and always be BTP Guidance Framework compliant.

SAP Build Code gives you all the design time services, runtime services, application frameworks, and generative AI that you would need to build, test, deploy, maintain, and monitor your cloud-native applications.

SAP Build Code

What’s the difference between SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Build Code in terms of IDE? Is it just Joule?

It is true that the SAP Build Code Development Environment is SAP Business Application Studio. This means that when you use the design time of SAP Build Code, you get all the great features of SAP Business Application Studio.

And you get some exclusive features that are only available in SAP Build Code. These are:

  1. Joule, the generative AI assistant, that helps you write code by providing natural language prompts.
  2. Centralized project management via the SAP Build Lobby – the single-entry point for all SAP Build product offerings (SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Code).
  3. Guided development – tutorials embedded with the SAP Build Code Development environment that assist you in creating your cloud-native applications and accomplish other tasks.
  4. High productivity tools that help you jumpstart your project development and ease the tasks of debugging, deploying, checking code into source control, and even creating and running your CICD pipelines. The cherry on the cake – the graphical modelers generate code files (with no bloat code), so that at any point of time, you can look at and edit the code that has been generated.

Why move to SAP Build Code from SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Web IDE?

SAP Web IDE was a great web-based IDE to develop SAP Fiori Freestyle apps for Neo and CF, SAP Fiori elements app, mobile and S/4HANA extensions. However, SAP Web IDE is already in deprecation and will no longer be available for renewal after December 31st, 2028. A large majority of SAP Web IDE customers have migrated to SAP Business Application Studio and SAP Build Code already.

SAP Business Application Studio is the next generation of SAP Web IDE and gives you a lot of innovative features to develop your extensions or custom applications. As mentioned above, it is also the IDE within SAP Build Code.

SAP Build Code gives you the following advantages:

  1. Simplified provisioning and onboarding experience (via the booster)
  2. Interoperability between pro-code and low-code tools, fostering fusion team development between professional developers and business experts.
  3. Combines runtime and design time capabilities with improved developer experience via visual tools and Joule, SAP’s generative AI assistant
  4. Simplified buying experience and resource-based consumption model.

You can also refer to our roadmap to see all the great, new features that we have in store for you. To migrate from SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code, you can follow this blog.

Until next time, happy coding!

 

 

SAP Build Code availability may differ from region to region.

entry.title: Migrate Content from SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
If you want to use all the features of SAP Build Code, the easiest way currently is to run the SAP Build Code booster (booster tutorial). The booster will create a new subaccount for you. But maybe you already have a subaccount with SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)? So you probably need to migrate your content to the new subaccount. But that is pretty easy. The only prerequisite is that you have added your BAS project from the old subaccount to a git repository. You can use the simplified git for that. You can find an example part of the full-stack tutorial

Now you can just clone the project to the new SAP Build Lobby.

1. Go to your new subaccount with the SAP Build Code entitlement and open the lobby by select your SAP Build Code instance.

2. Afterwards you just have to select Clone in the SAP Build Lobby.

3. Select the project type, like Full-Stack Application, and provide the Git repository URL and Project Name. As a last step just select your preferred Dev Space and press Create.

Now your project is also available in SAP Build Code:

To free up some space you can delete your old project on the old BAS subaccount or if you have migrated all projects you can delete the old subaccount, if you don’t need it anymore of other purposes.

Additional Information:

Did you know that you can also share your project with other developers directly from the lobby? Just select the menu of your project and select “Manage Members”.

And add the additional developers with their email:

Happy Coding!

entry.title: Unleashing Centralized Digital Experience for Suppliers – SAP Build Workzone
entry.content:
A Wave of Modern Digital Workplace

In today's business landscape, digitalization and innovation pose significant challenges for companies. To meet the ever-evolving needs of customers, businesses must swiftly adopt cloud solutions. However, progress is often hindered by limited resources and the complexities of maintaining IT infrastructures with multiple applications. To overcome these challenges, companies need to adopt digital processes, reduce technological obstacles, and empower both experts and citizen developers to drive innovation. 

This blog post will explore how SAP Build Workzone empowers companies to unleash the full potential of their central digital workplace, creating a more connected and efficient ecosystem for suppliers.


Background & Business Challenges

Customer is committed to becoming an Intelligent Enterprise by leveraging the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). To achieve this, they are dedicated to modernizing and replacing outdated business processes, empowering IT professionals and business users with tools to efficiently create engaging business sites for employees, partners, suppliers, and customers. A suitable replacement for the current landscape of supplier resources and applications is imperative. The scattered information throughout the enterprise lacks a centralized access point, causing inefficiencies in navigating through numerous disconnected systems. Decentralized content creation further hinders the development of flexible business sites and pages. The customer’s objective is to boost user productivity and engagement through interactive workspaces, forums, and knowledge-based articles. By centralizing information and facilitating efficient content creation, the company aims to create a seamless and productive environment for all users.

 

Customer's Vision to Elevate Supplier Engagement

The vision for the Supplier Portal is the ultimate centralized platform for Vendors/Suppliers and external partners. The aim is to provide easy access to all necessary resources in one location, including the Supplier Handbook, Supplier Resources, Proxied Applications, Contingent Worker Resources, and the option to access the Restricted Supplier Portal content. The customer worked with the SAP MaxAttention team to create a Digital Workplace that is agile and consistent with SAP's user experience. In this process, the team incorporated the latest innovations of BTP to provide a better user experience and easier content management. After a successful proof-of-concept, the team confidently deployed the updated content for productive usage through internal and external user onboarding processes

 

SAP MaxAttention Engagement

The success of this project is a true testament to SAP MaxAttention’s expertise and commitment to delivering exceptional results. During this engagement, SAP MaxAttention team worked along with SAP Build Work Zone Product Development and the customer’s CoE teams. This collaboration resulted in quick ROI and increased value by maximizing existing SAP solutions, specifically in the space of SAP Build subscription. SAP Build is powered by SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) which enables business experts to easily create and augment enterprise applications, automate tedious processes, and design engaging business sites by using visual tools and a rich set of services and pre-built content. SAP Build capabilities include:

  • SAP Build Code – SAP Build Code provides generative AI-based code development with Joule copilot, optimized for Java and JavaScript application development. It offers a turn-key environment for coding, testing, integrations, and application lifecycle management.
  • SAP Build Apps – Evolution of AppGyver to build mobile and web applications with a pixel-perfect design using the drag-and-drop functionality and a rich component library.
  • SAP Build Process Automation – Offers task and process automation leveraging robotic process automation (RPA) bots, workflows, forms, embedded artificial intelligence (AI), business rules, and decisions.
  • SAP Build Work Zone – Allows the creation of enterprise-grade custom business sites using new capabilities such as a flexible page builder and context-aware integrated UI cards.

Architecture

Below is the architecture of the digital Supplier Portal in SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition.

Delivery Scope

Setup of SAP Build Work Zone

For the platform preparation, we started with onboarding activities in SAP BTP, for example, Sub-account setup, setting up SAML Trust with PingIDP for authentication, user management & provisioning, and authorization management, data privacy, workspace setup to configure areas and workspace.

Building a Central Entry Point (Home Page)

The company's home page has been redesigned and now serves as the landing page for the website, featuring protected content. The customer team has been given the authority to distinguish between the administrative aspects of the SAP Build Work Zone landing page and to use pre-built templates and layouts to implement best practices. The objective is to differentiate between the usage of different widgets and to decide whether to build a Workspace or leave the content on the Home Page.

Supplier Handbook

Supplier Handbook workspace hosts a collection of key resources and assets (e.g., revision-controlled documents) used by customer’s Vendors/Suppliers. Private workspace access is only for onboard suppliers. Some of the key requirements were to have an intuitive user experience, manage contents and their versions, and send communication to the stakeholders whenever changes are made to the contents. 

Supplier Resources

Supplier Resources is a public workspace that hosts a collection of resources and assets needed for supplier onboarding. The customer’s prospective and established suppliers can find all the publicly available documents, process documentation, and answers to frequently asked questions related to the customer’s Account Payable, Invoicing Process, etc. This workspace also provides an entry point to the Supplier Handbook that provides access to protected resources.

Just-In-Time Reports Integration via SAP Build Process Automation

One of the requirements of the project is to enable vendors to view stocking reports in real-time. To achieve this, we leveraged SAP Build Process Automation.  SAP Build Process Automation is a citizen developer solution to adapt, improve, and innovate business processes with no-code workflow management and robotic process automation capabilities.
Using this tool, we developed unattended BOT to publish reports from BI and S/4HANA. On a periodical basis, automatic synch between the FTP and the SAP Build Work Zone is triggered. This business requirement was prototyped using SAP Build Work Zone OData API. The below diagrams illustrate the technical architecture of the solution.  

Supplier Registration Forms

To fulfill the need for supplier diversity, the existing registration form for suppliers to request access to a private workspace contains various form controls. The form includes multipage reports, and dynamic content, and retains automatic answers to meet the key requirements.

Outcomes

Empowered Business Users with Enterprise-Grade Business Sites

SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition enables business users to boost productivity and engagement by creating digital workplace solutions. This platform provides a central entry point for accessing business processes, applications, communication, and information from any device.

Business Value:

  • Improve productivity by accessing apps, processes, and information all in one place.
  • Experience a seamlessly integrated environment across SAP and third-party apps to accommodate collaboration tools and integrated information
  • With SAP Build Work Zone as a collaboration platform, users will have all the resources needed to make informed decisions faster
  • Say goodbye to clunky workflows, and hello to a world of efficient business apps, reports, and information that promote collaboration and drive success

Key Milestones:

  • Project Duration: < 4 months
  • Unified and Intelligent work experience by providing central access to apps, tasks, notifications, information, and communication.              
  • Optimize Business Processes with employees, partners, customers & suppliers.
  • Reduce TCO by leveraging existing investments in the SAP Business Technology Platform and harmonization of existing systems and repositories.
  • Integrated collaboration capabilities that can be extended to other business processes
  • Seamless SAP and third-party apps integration
  • Establish Central Inbox

Following are some of the business KPIs:

Contact persons:

SAP MaxAttention CoE: Kiran Kola, Rohit Dwivedi 

SAP Product Management: Florian Buech

entry.title: SAP Fiori development newsletter July 2024 (issue #29)
entry.content:
This is our first issue since SAP Sapphire, so we’ll feature some of the innovations announced at that event. AI was everywhere, with the goal of making users more productive in solutions across SAP’s portfolio. SAP Build Code is great example of a product that integrates AI to make application development faster and easier.

We are hosting our second SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago on August 13. Please join us to see the latest innovations in our portfolio, including how AI makes building SAP Fiori apps easier and a sneak preview of some planned functionality to make business users more efficient when using SAP Fiori apps.

If you want to meet with fellow developers and the SAP engineers who build our professional development tools, I encourage you to join our monthly SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools roundtable group.
Our next meeting is August 14, 2024.
Learn about the group at https://blogs.sap.com/2021/07/31/peer-to-peer-learning-at-the-sap-fiori-elements-and-sap-fiori-tools-roundtable/
You can also email me if you would like an invitation.

If you prefer to receive this in your inbox, please subscribe to the SAP Fiori development newsletter.

Development News

Sapphire 2024 summary from a user experience perspective

Every session I attended included some discussion on how artificial intelligence will make the product experience better. This summary focuses on how to implement the SAP Fiori user experience as customers transition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-sapphire-2024-highlights-on-ux-application-development-and-ai/ba-p/13723602

 

What’s new for application development, automation and integration on SAP BTP

SAP Build Code and BTP were everywhere at Sapphire, especially in the context of building applications that follow clean core principles.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-sapphire-2024-what-s-new-for-application-development-automation-and/ba-p/13736396

 

Streamlining data entry: exploring the new 'paste' feature in SAP Fiori elements V4 tables

The recent release of SAP Fiori elements for OData V4 introduces the ability to paste data into tables, much like you would in Microsoft Excel. This enhancement significantly elevates the user experience for Fiori elements V4 application users.

https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/streamlining-data-entry-exploring-the-new-paste-feature-in-fiori-elements/ba-p/13710973

 

SAP Fiori tools adds tighter integration with ABAP Development Tools

Developers can now have a more integrated experience when working with ADT at the backend and SAP Fiori tools for front-end development You don’t need to manually switch between the tooling environments and remember which system and entity they wish to use. Now, with one click in ADT, you can seamlessly switch from ABAP to SAP Fiori development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-may-2024-release-adds-closer-integration-with-abap/ba-p/13720761

 

SAP Fiori tools 2406 includes added support for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and more

Developers can now seamlessly create and deploy applications to SAP S/4HANA Cloud directly from Visual Studio Code.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-2406-update-includes-added-support-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13743708

 

Copy and paste like a pro with SAPUI5 tables

Maximizing the efficiency of business users through advanced Copy & Paste: SAPUI5 1.124 improves copy/paste operations in tables, letting business users work in a more natural way with improved productivity.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sapui5-tables-maximizing-efficiency-through-advanced-copy-amp-paste/ba-p/13723158

 

Create an app variant to introduce functionality without changing the original app

You can extend SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP BTP, ABAP Environment with an application variant, without changing the original application.

https://help.sap.com/docs/bas/developing-sap-fiori-app-in-sap-business-application-studio/extending-sap-fiori-application-for-s-4hana-cloud

 

Announcing UI5 Web Components 2.0

UI5 Web Components 2.0 enhances performance, reliability, and sets the stage for future innovations for web development.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/blast-off-ui5-web-components-2-0-is-now-live/ba-p/13744227

 

Reality check on applying clean code principles in SAPUI5 development

Applying clean code principles to SAPUI5 development is essential for building maintainable, scalable, and robust applications. Real-world constraints can make it challenging to follow these principles perfectly. By understanding the constraints and striving for continuous improvement, developers can still achieve a high standard of code quality.

https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs-about-sap-websites/applying-clean-code-principles-in-sap-ui5-development-reality-check/ba-p/13739199

 

Product updates for SAP Business Application Studio – June edition

As we cross the halfway mark of the year, it’s the perfect time to reflect on our progress and introduce the exciting new features we've added to BAS over the past few months.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-business-application-studio-june-edition/ba-p/13721825

Events

SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago – August 13

After our successful event in Dublin. Ireland, we are hosting another SAP Fiori Innovation Day in Chicago. By attending this event, you will learn:

  • SAP’s UX strategy and how it is evolving
  • Recent UX innovations that fulfill the promise of the intelligent, collaborative enterprise
  • Making SAP Fiori work for you – finding the apps you need and adapting them to your business requirements
  • Building apps using the latest tools and technologies – the same ones SAP uses to build apps for SAP S/4HANA (including optional hands-on workshop)
  • How to move your organization forward, including how to take advantage of AI for developing applications

To bring you this content at no charge, this event is sponsored by Mindset Consulting, Convergent IS, and Sovanta.

Register today: https://events.sap.com/noam/sap-fiori-innovation-day-chicago-2024/en/home

Watch the recordings from re>≡CAP 2024 and UI5con 2024

Missed our community conferences around our programming frameworks CAP and SAPUI5? No worries! All sessions were recorded and are now available online.
Go to re>≡CAP event page to access recordings
Go to UI5con event page to access recordings

Tutorials

openSAP content has moved to the learning.sap.com platform

Developing and Extending SAP Fiori Elements Apps

https://learning.sap.com/courses/developing-and-extending-sap-fiori-elements-apps

Developing an SAP Fiori Elements App Based on a CAP OData V4 Service

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-an-sap-fiori-elements-app-based-on-a-cap-odata-v4-service

Adapting the UI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Using SAP Screen Personas

https://learning.sap.com/courses/adapting-the-ui-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-using-sap-screen-personas

 

Tutorial: Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 applications

To extend SAPUI5 applications, SAPUI5 Flexibility offers capabilities not only for developers, but also for key users and end users using simple visual / WYSIWYG tools..

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/new-tutorial-mission-key-user-adaptation-for-sapui5-applications/ba-p/13639694

 

Tutorial: SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio are not new. What is new, is the support for SAP S/HANA Cloud. This enables developers to enhance SAPUI5 applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with features that were previously only for on-premise customers. See this blog post linking to an extensive, end-to-end tutorial:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/extending-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-using-sapui5-adaptation-projects/ba-p/13607813

Back issues from the past year

SAP FIori development newsletter #28 (May 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #27 (March 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #26 (January 2024)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #25 (November 2023)

SAP Fiori development newsletter #24 (September 2023)

entry.title: See how SAP Enterprise Support helps customers use SAP Build to empower their digital transformation
entry.content:
SAP Build is a comprehensive suite of low-code and pro-code tools designed to accelerate application development and automation with the power of generative AI. It is built on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) from SAP that helps accelerate development and automation. SAP Build is an enabler for everyone no matter the skill level to create and enhance enterprise applications, process automations, and business sites, all with drag-and-drop simplicity. 

Consisting of three components SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Work Zone, it empowers more users to develop irrespective of their skill level thereby lessening the burden on professional developers.

SAP Build Code is a comprehensive application development environment tailored for developers working with Java and JavaScript. The turnkey solution combines runtime and design time capabilities with built-in enterprise solution integrations, DevOps, and Application Lifecycle Management. Joule, the AI copilot that understands your business, is available to assist with code generation.

In this blog, we are elaborating the assets which are available to provide foundational Support to customers as part of their SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition subscription to use the SAP Build portfolio to empower their digital transformation. 

With SAP Enterprise Support, customers can:

  • Receive guidance and realize value through AI-powered services, best practice content, remote quality checks, and assessment services. 
  • Empower your team to gain knowledge and skills and collaborate with experts for answers to support-related questions.
  • Transform your end-to-end landscape with application lifecycle management solutions to deploy, operate, and optimize cloud and hybrid business solutions.

If you want to just gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio we have provided this SAP Build Big Picture session. In this e-learning, you will gain an overview of the SAP Build portfolio including SAP Build Code. It discusses the importance of the Clean Core concept and explore the low code/no code tools available for building new applications. Additionally, a high-level overview of SAP Build Code application development, assisted by Joule, is provided. For more detail, we have provided all the overview sessions for the SAP Build portfolio in the learning program Learn to develop using low-code through SAP Build provided through the SAP Enterprise Support Value Map program.

Below we have listed the SAP Build components and the corresponding support provided to customers to get started:

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Process Automation powers the automation of workflows and tasks with comprehensive workflow management, robotic process automation, and AI capabilities.  

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Process Automation. This includes an introductory session which has a demo the tool in action. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Process Automation. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Process Automation. In the first step, we give an overview of process automation in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Process Automation and customers learn how to create workflows, decision management, and task automation with powerful yet intuitive no-code capabilities through the configuration of a specific business process to approve Sales Orders automatically. Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps is an evolution of SAP AppGyver and it enables users to build web and mobile enterprise applications.  With hundreds of templates and pre-built business logic components, you can jumpstart your application projects and reduce development time. 

To gain an overview to SAP Build Process Automation we have created a Discovery Center mission on Foundational support to start with SAP Build Apps. This includes an introductory session. Also it includes a multi-day Expert Guided Implementation service on SAP Build Apps. The purpose of this EGI service is to ramp-up customers to understand and adopt SAP Build Apps. In the first step, we give an overview of application development in the context of SAP Build. Afterwards we show case the capabilities of SAP Build Apps and customers learn how to create mobile and web applications, connect to SAP systems, and deploy and run on SAP Business Technology Platform.​ Further information including how to register for the service is provided here.

SAP Build Work Zone

SAP Build Work Zone, brings the capabilities of SAP Launchpad and SAP Work Zone into one solution, facilitating the rapid creation of engaging and personalized business sites. It provides centralized access to business application information on any device.

To give an overview on SAP Build Work Zone, we have created the e-learning Explore the power of SAP Build Work Zone - SAP BTP Adoption Tutorial which provides a comprehensive Central Entry Point Digital experience. It encompasses an introduction to essential tools such as SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center, and SAP Mobile Start. Key topics are discussed including the capabilities of SAP Build Work Zone, both standard and advanced editions and Identity and Access management through the SAP Cloud Identity Service. Also, we take a deep dive into the One Inbox suite quality of the SAP Task Center. and SAP Mobile Start as your native mobile entry point.

To provide further information for SAP Build Work Zone, we have created additional e-learnings. Firstly, we give an overview to integrate SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and SAP Mobile Start. We walk you through the configuration process for the SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition ensuring you gain a comprehensive understanding of its setup and functionalities. Also, you will gain proficiency in integrating SAP Mobile Start with SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

Also, we have provided an e-learning for SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition Deep Dive to effectively configure, deploy, and manage SAP Build Work Zone environments, leveraging its capabilities to enhance collaboration and productivity within your organization. The content shows how to set up SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition with integration of IAS (Identity Authentication Service) and IPS (Identity Provisioning Service) for user access, the process of creating a workspace and adding content such as How-to documents and technical support access, developing a UI Card in SAP Business Application Studio and deploying it to the workspace and integrating the Launchpad service and adding exposed content from an SAP S/4HANA system.

SAP Build Code

Apart from the SAP Build Big Picture session which provides an overview to SAP Build Code, we are piloting a new Expert Guided Implementation service on Cloud Application Programming Development using SAP Build Code. The purpose of this service is to empower customers with the knowledge of CAP development in SAP HANA Cloud and provide customers with the knowledge to develop and design in SAP HANA Cloud utilizing the SAP Business Application studio using SAP Build Code and the SAP AI co-pilot Joule. To find out more about this pilot through SAP Enterprise Support Advisory Council, please reach out directly.

The e-learning Clean Core with SAP Business Technology Platform offers valuable insights to understand how SAP Business Technology Platform can help cultivate a cleaner and more sustainable core for SAP systems. It offers insights into how SAP BTP can support the fundamental pillars of data, integration, and extensibility. It explores the topic of extensibility in-depth and how the various components of SAP Business Technology Platform support the building of extensions in line with clean core principles.

For customers, if you have issues accessing the above links, there may be an authorizations issue. Please note a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required for most of the above assets. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

For internal colleagues, if you cannot access the above links, please use the following link, click on “Access” and use the above direct link once access has been granted.

We hope that this range of content and assets will support you in your digital transformation. For any queries in relation to any of the above assets, please reach out to us directly.

Thanks and Regards,

Robert Kelly

SAP BTP Lead Architect – SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 24.7
entry.content:
I am happy to announce that a new release of the Mobile Development Kit(MDK) is available for all Mobile Services customers and can be downloaded on the SAP Software Center (and also available on community Download page).

The SAP Mobile Services Client 24.7.0 will soon be available in the Apple App Store and Google Play Store.

SAP Mobile Development Kit enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / VSCode extension) and run it natively on Mobile (Android & iOS).  

Below enhancements are available only on iOS and Android platforms:

  • New UI enhancements
    • Cards Collection Control
    • Support Column Specific Editing in Data Table Control
    • Support Multiple Columns in a Form Cell Section
    • Caption Functionality for Note FormCell Control
    • Enable Multiple Attachments Selection in Attachment control
    • Styling Support for Banner Message and Progress Banner Actions
    • Additional Styling Support for Images and Icons
  • API and Syntax enhancements
    • Access OData Service Metadata Document ($metadata)
    • Streamlined Filter Criteria to OData Conversion
    • Access the Instance of an Extension Control
    • Refresh Metadata
    • Access Section Header and its items
    • Proxy class for Button Table, Section Header and Section Footer

Cards Collection Control
With this release, we are introducing a new UI control: Cards collection. A card collection provides brief, related pieces of information and serves as an entry point, teaser, or preview to more detailed content. By tapping on a card, users can select it and navigate to a dedicated page with comprehensive information.
The contents of a card are categorized into several areas:

  • Media: is an optional decorative element that allows you to include an image that matches the card context. 
  • Header: allows for a quick overview of key information, such as a title, subtitle, or status of the card. When using the card header, only include essential information about the card’s content. An action button within the card header can be used for action that directly impacts the card, for example, to favorite, bookmark, filter the card, or display a pop-up menu displaying additional options.
  • Extended Header: allows up to three additional rows below the Header. Each row can contain one of the following
    • Labels
    • Text
    • Rating
    • Tags
  • Footer: is the bottom of a card that can contain two buttons (Primary and Secondary).

The card system offers enhanced customization and flexibility, including adaptable sizing options to fit various form factors and card layouts such as:

  • Vertical: display the cards in vertical list.
  • Horizontal Scroll: display the cards in horizontally scrollable list. Optionally, you can indicate that all cards should display with same height using the UseSameHeight property.
  • Masonry: display cards in grid-like structure with flexible arrangement with each card adapting to the available space.
    For more information, refer to Card Collection.

    Cards Collection on iOS and Android

Support Column Specific Editing in Data Table control
The MDK Data Table control now supports column specific editing in Data Table control. You can now set the EditType property to None for a column. That particular column will not be editable while other columns are editable. You can also use a rule to programmatically set cell-specific editing in the table.

Editable Data Table on iOS and Android

Support Multiple Columns in a Form Cell Section
You can now define a multi-column layout in a FormCell Section displaying more FormCell controls in defined columns on the page.

 

 

 

"Layout": {
                "NumberOfColumns": 3,
                "MinimumInteritemSpacing": 20
            }

 

 

 

Please note below behavior of the NumberOfColumns property

  • On iOS, this setting is only valid in regular mode (iPad or large iPhone Landscape). In compact mode it will always be set to 1 column.
  • On Android, this setting is only valid when current screen width is larger than 600dp. When the current screen width (e.g. in phone portrait mode) is less than 600dp, it will always be set to 1 column.
    Multi Column FormCell on iOS and Android

Caption Functionality for Note FormCell Control
Note FormCell control has been enhanced to support Caption and its associated styling options. For more information, refer to this document.

 

 

 

{
    "Caption": "Note's Caption",
    "_Type": "Control.Type.FormCell.Note",
    "_Name": "FormCellControl",
    "IsVisible": true,
    "Separator": true,
    "PlaceHolder": "Description",
    "HelperText": "Please enter description",
    "MaxNumberOfLines": 8,
    "MinNumberOfLines": 2,
    "Enabled": true,
}

 

 

 

Enable Multiple Attachments selection in Attachment Control
Attachment FormCell has been enhanced to allow users to select multiple files at once. This enhancement applies to both iOS and Android platforms.
Adding multiple attachments on iOS and Android

Styling support for Banner Message and Progess Banner actions
You can now style the BannerMessage and Progress Banner actions with newly introduced styling properties:.

  • Semantic: can be used to apply semantic colors to the banner such as Normal (default), Tint, Positive, Negative and warning.
  •  Styles: can be used to customize the banner end-to-end.

You can also style the different states (Progress/Completion) of the ProgressBanner individually.

Banner Styling on iOS and Android

 

Progress Banner Styling on iOS and Android

Please note that the default style for BannerMessage has been updated: text and stripe color are now grey on iOS, and text color is grey on Android. Find more information, refer to the styling section for Banner Message and Progress Banner actions.

Additional Styling support for Icons and Images
With this release, we support background-color styling option for images and icons in the following MDK controls:

  • ObjectCell
  • ObjectCollectionCell
  • GridTable
  • ObjectHeader
  • KPIHeader
  • ProfileHeader
  • ContactCell
  • ObjectCard
  • CardCollection (iOS)
  • Button (iOS)
  • Image section
  • ImageCollection

Accessing OData Service Metadata Document ($metadata)
We have added new methods to access the OData service metadata document file (.xml).  This file can be used in the application to augment the business logic such as:

  • Dynamically show or hide OData properties based on an attribute value.
  • Dynamically show or hide OData properties based on an annotation value.
  • Checking max length of field

The getMetadata() API will return a new CSDLDocumentProxy, through which you can access the $metadata of both Offline and Online services.  Once you have this proxy, you can execute the following methods via the proxy:

  • getOriginalText(): returns the original $metadata content as XML string. You will need to first define the retainOriginalText CSDL option in the. Service file.

 

 

 

Export default function getOriginalText(context) {
//get OData service provider    
let provider = context.getODataProvider('/MDK2407/Services/SampleServiceV4.service');
    let csdlProxy = provider.getMetadata();
    if (csdlProxy) {
        alert('OriginalText for OData Service is: ' + csdlProxy.getOriginalText());
    } else {
        alert('Service is not initialised!');
    }
  }​

 

 

 

To enable this, you need to define the following CSDLOptions:

 

 

 

//Offline 
{
    "DestinationName": "SampleServiceV4",
    "OfflineEnabled": true,
    "OfflineOptions": {
        "CSDLOptions": [
            "retainOriginalText",
            "retainResolvedText"
        ]
    }
}​

//Online 
{
    "DestinationName": "SampleServiceV4Online",
    "OfflineEnabled": false,
    "OnlineOptions": {
        "CSDLOptions": [
            "retainOriginalText",
            "retainResolvedText"
        ]
    }

 

 

 

  • getCompactXML():  returns the $metadata content as compact XML string.
  • getResolvedText(): returns the resolved $metadata content as XML string with inline references and expanded aliases. You will need to first define the retainResolvedText CSDL option in the. service file.
  • getVersionCode(): returns code for the OData version. If the OData version is considered as a decimal number (e.g. 4.0), then multiplying by 100 will give the code (e.g. 400)
  • getVersionText(): returns text for the OData service version, e.g. “4.0”

Update Cached Metadata
We have added a new API refreshMetadata() that checks the compatibility between the current and the $metadata from the backend server, then updates the $metadata if there is no conflict.

This API returns a promise with a boolean result indicating whether the $metadata was updated successfully. Any $metadata changes happening on the backend server after initializing the data service for the first time will not be reflected unless the users call the refreshMetadata API.

Streamlined Filter Criteria to OData Conversion

  • When working with Filters on a Section Table, you can now easily convert an array of filter criteria to the OData filter and sorter string representations via below APIs. getFilterActionResult(): returns the content of _filter which should be the resolved OData $filter query string. (null if there's no filter)
  • getSorterActionResult(): that return the content of _sorter which should be the resolved OData $orderby query string (null if there's no sorter)

Access the instance of an Extension Control

  • Expose a new API to allow users to access the extension control instance of the ExtensionFormCell. This instance allows the MDK application developer to call the extension methods if needed to adjust the behavior of the extension control at runtime.
    • Previously, the users could only access the extension control instance of the ExtensionFormCell through internal properties. Now, this access is officially supported by MDK through the new getExtension() API in the new ExtensionFormCellProxy class.
  • Enhance the existing getExtensions() API of SectionProxy to make it clearer to users
    • Introduced three other new proxy classes: ExtensionSectionProxy, ObjectCollectionSectionProxy, ObjectHeaderSectionProxy. They are all subclasses of the existing SectionProxy
    • ExtensionSectionProxy will have the getExtension() API, as there can be only 1 extension based on the metadata definition
    • ObjectCollectionSectionProxy & ObjectHeaderSectionProxy will have the getExtensions() API, as the extensions are defined as an array in the metadata definition
    • Existing getExtensions() API from the SectionProxy will now be marked as deprecated and always return an empty array.

Proxy Class for Button Table, Section Header and Section Footer
To allow better access of specific controls when executing rules, we are introducing below new Proxy Classes:

  • ButtonTableProxy
  • ButtonTableButtonProxy
  • SectionHeaderProxy
  • SectionHeaderButtonProxy
  • SectionHeaderIconProxy
  • SectionHeaderLabelProxy
  • SectionFooterProxy

These classes support the following methods:

  • getName
  • getType
  • redraw

Additionally, all control and section proxy classes support the getParent method.

 

 

 

const className = context.constructor.name;
const name = context.getName();
const type = context.getType();
const parent = context.getParent();

 

 

 

Access to Section Header Items
Enhanced access to the Section Header items. This includes methods to enable/disable and hide/show items in a section header with new API functions:

  • SectionProxy
    • getHeader()
  • SectionHeaderItemProxy
    • getVisible()
    • setVisible()
  • SectionHeaderButtonItemProxy (Inherits SectionHeaderItemProxy methods)
    •  getEnabled()
    • setEnabled()

New to MDK development?
Follow these tutorials to get started and learn more about Mobile development kit!
I am looking forward to your feedback/comments.

entry.title: UI5 Tooling 4.0
entry.content:
UI5 Tooling 4.0

The UI5 Core team is pleased to announce the release of UI5 Tooling 4.0.

This release has a strong focus on enabling UI5 2.x compatibility for projects. In addition, along with some important housekeeping, we introduced a new feature for automatically generating locale information in the manifest.json.

We are doing our best to minimize upgrade efforts for UI5 developers. Projects currently working with UI5 Tooling 3.x are generally expected to work with UI5 Tooling 4.0. More on this below.

Node.js and npm Requirements

UI5 Tooling 4.0 requires Node.js v20.11.0, v22.0.0, or higher.

This allows us to make use of the latest Node.js features and to upgrade dependencies requiring current Node.js versions.

Specification Version 4.0

This new major Specification Version comes with breaking changes that might have an impact on projects. Please carefully read the migration guide before applying the new version to your projects.

These changes are mostly related to how bundles are created in UI5 Tooling. Even if you do not use custom bundles, this might have an impact on the initial loading behavior of your application or library. These changes pave the road for future improvements on bundling in UI5. Stay tuned for more!

UI5 2.x

UI5 Tooling 4.0 along with the new Specification Version 4.0 is required for projects to utilize UI5 2.x.

When defining Specification Version 4.0 in your project, the code generated by UI5 Tooling will apply all the current best practices, such as consistently loading resources asynchronously and terminating the use of "eval()" (a common CSP violation). Both of which are prerequisites for projects to be compatible with UI5 2.x.

New Feature: Automatic Generation of Locale Information

In your application or library project, UI5 Tooling now analyzes existing manifest.json files. For any "i18n" and "ResourceModel" configuration, this ensures that the "supportedLocales" property is defined. If it is not defined, it is now filled in automatically based on the translation files present in the project.

This improves user experience by avoiding unnecessary requests at runtime. Without a defined set of "supported locales", the UI5 runtime has to determine the available translations by requesting individual files, often resulting in "404 Not Found" server responses.

Note that you can opt out of this behavior by defining the "supportedLocales" property yourself. The feature is active for both, the build and the server.

Learn more about this new feature in the builder documentation.

Upgrade Today

Upgrading to UI5 Tooling 4.0 is a straightforward task for most projects.

First, ensure that you meet the minimum required versions of Node.js and npm using the commands "node --version" and "npm --version". Make sure to also check your CI/CD workflows.

Then upgrade the UI5 CLI in your project by executing the following command:

npm i --save-dev @ui5/cli@latest

Also update your global install using:

npm i --global @ui5/cli@latest

You may already stop there, since updating the Specification Version remains optional.

However, if you want UI5 Tooling to apply the current best practices when building your project, also update the "specVersion" property in all ui5.yaml files to "4.0": 

 

specVersion: "4.0"
[...]

 

Afterwards, test your project as usual. In case any problems come up, make sure to check our migration guide. And if you need further assistance, we invite you to raise an issue on GitHub.

entry.title: Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

In our continuous efforts to enhance your application development experience, we're thrilled to introduce several new SAP Build Code features. Let me share with you some highlights of the latest releases.

 

Generative AI support for productivity tool

In SAP Build Code, our generative AI assistant Joule supports you with many developer tasks including generating data models and services, the application logic, and unit testing. We now allow you to use Joule where you need it: inside our productivity tools. You will find the little Joule icon is displayed besides the title of the Data Models and the Services tiles in the Storyboard. Opening Joule, you will see it supports you based on your current context. This means, when you are starting to enhance, for example, the data model Joule opens for exactly this purpose. You get an inline area, in which you can type your prompt. In addition, you will find a list of suggested prompts.

Joule icon in the data models and services tiles

Suggested prompts

 

Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services 

We got the request to allow developers to use Joule to modify their already generated SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) data model or service entities. I’m happy to tell you that it is now possible to modify either the data model (schema.cds) or the service entities (service.cds), or both, using a prompt, and get the enhanced cds content.

With this new feature, it is now possible to:

  • add or remove an entity,
  • add, modify, or remove properties of entities,
  • create or remove associations between entities,
  • get more support on the service projection definition.

Step 1: Create your data model and services with the help of Joule

Step 2: Use Joule to enhance your data model

Result: Enhanced data model

Please read the documentation for further information about Generative AI-Powered Development in SAP Build Code.

 

Guided development for SAP Fiori app development

With SAP Fiori elements you can build your applications according to your specific requirements. It is one of the options available to develop SAP Fiori apps based on SAPUI5. In the guide center panel of your SAP Fiori Application project, you can now find an embedded tutorial for SAP Fiori app development. This tutorial describes step by step how to develop a custom page based on the flexible programming model.

Tutorial: Develop an SAP Fiori app on BTP

 

Graphical editor for hdbgrants files

When working with HDI hdbgrants files are used to define the permissions and access rights for users and roles within an HDI container in the SAP HANA database. In SAP Business Application Studio, the SAP HANA tools offer a graphical editor for hdbgrants files providing a convenient way of implementing cross-container or cross-schema scenarios. The syntax of hdbgrants files can be quite complex, hence this graphical editor can ease the development of such scenarios. Note that it is also possible to use the code editor or to toggle between code and graphical editor, depending on your personal preferences.

Graphical editor for hdbgrants files

The source of the privileges is a service instance containing information that can be used to grant privileges in the database. The editor lists the available service instances that are bound to the current project. A user can select which service instance should be used for the current file.

Privileges in hdbgrants files can be specified for two users: the HDI container’s object owner and the application user that is used by the application to connect to the database at runtime. The two tabs at the top of the editor allow selecting either user.

Individual privileges can be assigned on the available object types: global roles, schema-local roles, system privileges, schema privileges, object privileges, and global object privileges. The privileges can be granted either with grant option or without grant option. Grants with grant option are required for building database objects on top of other database objects. Since database objects in an HDI container should only be created by HDI (by means of the object owner user) the object owner should usually be granted privileges with grant option, while the application user only needs to perform DML operations on database objects and does not need the privileges with grant option.

For an easy way of keeping the privileges of the object owner and the application user in sync the editor allows copying the current privileges of the object owner to the application user via the “Copy properties to application user” button. When using this convenience feature all privileges that the object owner has with grant option are copied to the application user without grant option in order to set up the recommended permission model.

 

Further readings

You are still using SAP Business Application Studio and want to know, why you should change to SAP Build Code? Then I recommend reading this blog post written by my colleague Rupa Maity:  Why SAP Build Code? Read this blog post written by my colleague Marc Huber to find out how to migrate content from SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code.

In addition, I want to draw you attention to two other great blog posts:

Collaborate on your SAP Build Code project with your teammates! written by Rupa Maity

How to Develop SAP MDK Applications in SAP Build Code written by Liat Borenstein

 

For further information on new features, please check our roadmaps: roadmap for SAP Build Code, SAPUI5 Road Map. And have a look at What's New in SAP Build Code, the What's New Viewer for SAPUI5 and the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Release Notes. If you want to stay up-to-date with news, learning resources, and product and strategy updates for SAP Build Code: follow our tag.

We look forward to seeing how you use these new capabilities to create innovative solutions and drive your projects forward. Stay tuned for more updates and happy coding! 😊

entry.title: Transform your phone’s camera into a next-gen input tool
entry.content:
The Scenario

Imagine you work at a busy international airport where shipments from global suppliers arrive daily. Recently, your country introduced a regulation requiring all airports to maintain a list of international suppliers using air freight to send parcels smaller than 1 m³. Your task is to extract supplier information directly from shipping documents such as invoices, packing lists, and courier bills, and record this information in an S4/HANA system set up by the national department of foreign trade.

 

Why Pen and Paper Won’t Cut It

Managing shipment details manually by noting them down on a piece of paper and digitizing your notes later is not just time-consuming but feels like something out of the second century AD! While creating a mobile app could eliminate the need for manual data entry, typing on a small screen can be cumbersome and prone to errors, especially with special characters often found in foreign languages.

 

A Cutting-Edge Solution

SAP Build Code on SAP Discovery Center

 

SAP Document Information Extraction Service on SAP Discovery Center

 

Instead, you can build a cutting-edge solution using SAP Build Code and SAP Document Information Extraction Service to build an application that lets you extract information from images. Let’s see how you can build this amazing experience.

 

Building the solution

Getting Started: Essential Tutorials

Although this blog won’t be a step-by-step tutorial, it will point you toward the essential resources and concepts to help you get started.

  • Complete the Tutorials:
    • Use Generative AI to Process Business Documents
    • Try Out the SAP BTP SDK Wizard for Android
      Note: You can also implement this mobile app using SAP BTP SDK for iOS or Mobile Development Kit (MDK).
 
Create  Integration with SAP Mobile Services

You can either use your own use-case or this supplier form. Based on your use-case, you need to create a schema on the SAP Document Information Extraction Service, similar to the example below. If you are using the provided supplier form, select "Invoice" as the Document Type when creating the schema. Adding a description helps the AI model contextually match the extracted results to the fields defined in the schema.

 
Seamless Integration with SAP Mobile Services

To safeguard your application from potential disruptions due to API changes, add your APIs to SAP Mobile Services instead of hardcoding them into your app.

  • Retrieve API Credentials:
    • In the Instances & Subscriptions section of your sub-account, locate the service instance for Document Information Extraction Service (e.g., default_document-information-extraction-trial).
    • Click on the key under credentials and note the following:
      • url
      • uaa → clientid
      • uaa → clientsecret
      • uaa → url

Note: If a key does not exist, you can create one.

  • Configure SAP Mobile Services:
    • Go to SAP Mobile Services and create a new destination in the Mobile Services Connectivity.

      Step

      Field Key

      Field Value

      Basic Info

      Destination Name

      dox.service.destination

      Basic Info

      URL

      <url>/document-information-extraction/v1/

      Destination Configuration

      SSO Mechanism

      OAuth2 Client Credentials

      Destination Configuration

      Token Service URL

      <uaa → url>/oauth/token?grant_type=client_credentials&response_type=token

      Destination Configuration

      Client ID

      <uaa → clientid>

      Destination Configuration

      Client Secret

      <uaa → clientsecret>

    • Test your configuration by using the “ping” and “launch in browser” actions for the newly added destination. You should see a blank page upon successful authentication.
      Note: If prompted, please provide valid credentials to authenticate yourself.
Building the experience in your app
  • Write code to capture an image and send it to the Document Information Extraction Service using this API: Upload Document API.
  • Write code to pre-populate an input form from results extracted from the uploaded image using this API: Get Result API.

For inspiration, please check out the Scan2InputPoC repository on GitHub.
Note: this project is a proof of concept created by the author and is not an official recommendation from SAP; it is provided as-is.

 

Testing the solution

 

 

 

 

 

Once you have built your application with the necessary logic and configuration, it's time for the fun part: testing it in different scenarios such as:

  • Lighting Conditions: Test in various lighting settings such as dim lighting, sunlight, and artificial light.
  • Document Angles: Position the document at different angles on a flat surface or vertically.
  • Special Characters: Test documents containing special characters not found in the English alphabet.
  • Handwritten Documents: Try scanning and processing handwritten documents to evaluate the application’s accuracy.

Here's a video demo of what the capability would look like: 

 

What Next?

As you can see, you can quickly build a solution using SAP BTP to address a demanding problem in an ever-changing world. This powerful technology can also be used for digitizing patient records in healthcare and capturing maintenance reports in field services. I am very eager for all of you to try this out and hear about your possible use cases in the comments section.

 

Important Links

  • Product Help: SAP Build Code | SAP Mobile Services | SAP Document Information Extraction Service
  • Get Started Tutorials: SAP BTP SDK for Android | SAP Document Information Extraction Service
  • API Reference: SAP Document Information Extraction Service | SAP BTP SDK for Android

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 25th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/07/25/sap-developer-news-july-25th-2024/

DESCRIPTION

ABAP CDS TechByte Series

  • ABAP CDS TechBytes – https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQC3HBShmlMaPu_nL--4f20z

SAP Build Code July Updates

  • Product Updates for SAP Build Code – July 2024 Edition https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/product-updates-for-sap-build-code-july-2024-edition/ba-p/13764559

July Developer Challenge reminder

  • Blog post (containing links to each of the tasks) https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/july-developer-challenge-quot-reverse-apis-quot/ba-p/13749653

UI5 Tooling 4.0 and Code Connect recordings:

  • UI5 Tooling 4.0: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/ui5-tooling-4-0/ba-p/13769578
  • reCAP Recordings: https://recap-conf.dev/
  • ui5Con Recordings: https://openui5.org/ui5con/germany2024/
  • ABAPConf Recordings: https://abapconf.org/abapconfeurope2024/

CAP #GoodToKnow Shorts Series

  • Modeling many-to-many associations #SAPCAP #GoodToKnow – YouTube https://www.youtube.com/shorts/yGg3YD1weIA
  • Series Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDZ18v94otZSJJrpcNkPPV9

================================================

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP CDS TechByte Series

1:01 SAP Build Code July Updates

2:48 July Developer Challenge reminder

3:58 UI5 Tooling 4.0 and Code Connect recordings

4:55 CAP #GoodToKnow Shorts Series 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Shilpa] Hello SAP developers, I'm sure by now you would have seen the new TechByte series on ABAP Core Data Services. If not, here's a quick recap. Myself and developer advocate Sheena will walk you through the basics of ABAP CDS, covering the overview and key functionality, how a virtual element with complex logic can be created, extend a CDS View Entity, restrict the data authorizations using CDS Access Control, govern the quality of CDS Views using unit test, and troubleshooting tools available to analyze the data of CDS Views. I will leave the link to the ten-part videos in the description. Not just that, the advanced features of ABAP CDS will be coming up, so stay tuned.

[Thomas] First shipped earlier this year, we have the introduction of SAP Build Code. This is the AI-powered latest version of SAP's development environment based upon the SAP Business Application Studio. This month we see enhancements to SAP Build Code, many of which are based right upon your feedback from initial use of Build Code. Most of the enhancements focus on the Joule AI integration.

First of all, we've made it easier to get to the Joule Assistant from within the storyboard and the graphical navigation tools of the CAP designer itself. The part that I'm most excited about is originally Joule integration in SAP Build Code was really focused on creating new CAP entities. So very useful when you're first starting your project. But we've heard your feedback and we've added a lot of new functionality that also lets you alter an existing CAP data model. So you can add new entities, add or remove associations, and make these changes to an existing model all using the same AI assistant that you've been using to start your projects. In addition to this major enhancement for the AI capabilities, we also see the addition of a guided assistant for Fiori development as well as a new graphical HDBGrants editor as well. So be sure to check out all the latest enhancements to SAP Build Code with the link in the show notes to the blog post that details them.

[DJ] The SAP Developer Challenge this month is well on the way. The challenge is called Reverse APIs, and it's all about you creating, creating CAP-based services and API endpoints for us to call and validate. We're about three quarters of the way through the challenge. There are 12 tasks altogether. So it sounds about right for this time of the month. So far, we've been digging into defining and implementing actions and functions in a couple of simple services across the REST protocol and the OData protocol adapters. And now we're moving more into a sort of Northwind-based service scenario, where we can try and flex our Odata muscles a bit more. It's not too late to get started. There's plenty of discussion and help in the SAP Community Platform for each of the tasks. So what are you waiting for? Head over to the SAP Community. The blog post link is in the description and get going.

[Nico] Hi everyone, and welcome to the SAP Developer News. The UI5 team just released version four of the UI5 tooling, which comes with some handy new features as well as paving the road for UI5 version 2.x, which is still in the works, but hopefully available soon. One of the new features is that the tooling can now generate the supported locales property of the manifest.json. So no more ugly 404s in production. Check out the blog post linked in the description for more information. On a related note, we would like to point our attention to the recordings of {CodeConnect}. They're now available on YouTube. So for UI5Con, reCAP, and ABAPConf, there are a lot of recordings available so you can check out the sessions on YouTube in your own time. Use the summer break wisely. All right, hope to see you soon, and bye.

[Thomas] I wanted to draw your attention to the recent series of YouTube Shorts, all with the tag #GoodToKnow. These are created by our fellow developer advocate, DJ Adams, and look at the basics of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model. They do it in short 60-second chunks, and all formatted for easy consumption on a mobile device. This makes it easy to learn on the go, or just for those of you looking for some basic, but great CAP knowledge that you can consume in small, bite-sized chunks. In the most recent episode, DJ talks about many-to-many associations in the Cloud Application Programming Model. It's a concept that I thought would be difficult to explain and understand in 60 seconds, but DJ does a perfect job of it. So be sure to check out that latest video and the entire Good to Know series if you wanna learn more about the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model.

entry.title: Understand CSRF handling in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
What is CSRF?

CSRF stands for Cross-Site Request Forgery. It is a type of malicious attack that tricks the victim into submitting a malicious request. It uses the identity and privileges of the victim to perform an undesired function on their behalf. For instance, this could involve changing an email address or password, or making a purchase. The attack can cause damage even if the exploited action is normally harmless because it's performed without the user's consent or knowledge.

What is the defense mechanism?

CSRF token is a security measure used in web applications to protect against CSRF attacks.

It is a unique, random value associated with a user's session and is typically embedded within a form or URL to ensure that the user is intentionally submitting the request. This token is checked by the server to verify the source of the request, preventing malicious parties from tricking a user's browser into making a request on their behalf.

This is usually taken care of by the browser from where the app is launched and accesses APIs.

CSRF tokens are mainly used for communication with the server. Before an app is allowed to execute a modify operation through an HTTP request such as POST, PUT, DELETE etc., a CSRF token is generated by the server to ensure the security of the operation.

Here is a brief process of how it is used:

  1. First, the app sends a GET request to fetch a CSRF token from the server.
  2. During the GET request, the server generates a new CSRF token and sends it back in the response headers (usually under "x-csrf-token").
  3. The app then uses this token in the header of every modification request.
  4. The server validates the CSRF token in every modifying request. If it's valid, the operation proceeds.
  5. If it's not valid or missing, the server returns a 403 (Forbidden) status, and the operation does not proceed.

In this way, the CSRF token ensures that every operation modifying data is secure, authenticated, and originated from a trusted source. It protects against CSRF attacks where an attacker might trick a victim into executing actions of the attacker's choosing.

Why do we need additional CSRF handling for SAP Build Apps?
